DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF EMILY KRAMER'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE; AND FOR SANCTIONS: THE MOTION IS CONTINUED UNTIL AFTER THE COURT DECIDES THE MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS, NOW SET FOR APRIL 21, 2021. REGARDLESS OF THE OUTCOME OF THAT MOTION, THIS MATTER IS BEST RESOLVED BY A DISCOVERY REFEREE. AMONG OTHER REASONS, THERE ARE MORE THAN A CUMULATIVE 750 PAGES CONTAINED IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENTS ALONE. THE PARTIES ARE ENCOURAGED TO AGREE TO A DISCOVERY REFEREE FOR PURPOSES OF THESE WRITTEN DISCOVERY REQUESTS PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 638; CRC 3.901(A). IF THE PARTIES DO NOT CONSENT, THE COURT MAY APPOINT A REFEREE ON THE MOTION OF EITHER PARTY. CCP SECTION 639(A)(5); CRC 3.920(A). JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT CARTA, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS. NO APPEARANCES, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CARTA, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY DISCOVERY AND OTHER PROCEEDINGS UNTIL AUGUST 30, 2021 IS DENIED. THIS ORDER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT RENEWING ITS REQUEST FOR A STAY, SHOULD PLAINTIFF PROVE UNABLE TIMELY TO RESPOND TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY REQUESTS, AND DOES NOT PRECLUDE EITHER PARTY FROM FILING DISCOVERY MOTIONS, INCLUDING A REQUEST FOR APPOINTMENT OF A DISCOVERY REFEREE. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Apr-21-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CARTA, INC.'s Motion To Stay Proceedings.

No appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Carta, Inc.'s motion to stay discovery and other proceedings until August 30, 2021 is denied. Plaintiff acknowledges that in January 2021, her counsel informed Defendant's counsel that she had been diagnosed with cancer, is receiving aggressive treatment, and had been advised by her physician that she would not be able to sit for a deposition until August. However, Plaintiff disputes Defendant's understanding that she cannot participate in any written discovery before August, and asserts that the parties can and should use the interim period to resolve their disputes regarding written discovery, including disputes relating to discovery requests that Plaintiff served on Defendant in September 2020. The Court declines Defendant's request that it utilize a stay as a means of managing the discovery process. This order is without prejudice to Defendant renewing its request for a stay, should Plaintiff prove unable timely to respond to written discovery requests, and does not preclude either party from filing discovery motions, including a request for appointment of a discovery referee.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

PLAINTIFF EMILY KRAMER's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set One; And For Sanctions:

The Motion is continued until after the Court decides the Motion to stay proceedings, now set for April 21, 2021. Regardless of the outcome of that Motion, this matter is best resolved by a discovery referee. Among other reasons, there are more than a cumulative 750 pages contained in the separate statements alone. The parties are encouraged to agree to a discovery referee for purposes of these written discovery requests pursuant to CCP section 638; CRC 3.901(a). If the parties do not consent, the Court may appoint a referee on the motion of either party. CCP section 639(a)(5); CRC 3.920(a).

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-20-2021 Continued To Mar-17-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF EMILY KRAMER'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Court rules on Plaintiff Emily Kramer's demurrer to the answer to the complaint of Defendant Carta, Inc. as follows:

1. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the First [failure to state a claim], Fourth [no causation], and Fifth [causation] affirmative defenses because they fail to state facts sufficient to constitute a defense. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 430.20(a).) Affirmative defenses inject new matters into the case, rather than simply being responsive to essential allegations of the complaint. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 431.30(b)(2); Walsh v. West Valley Mission Comm. Coll. Dist. (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 1532, 1545-1546; Houk v. Williams Bros. (1943) 58 Cal.App.2d 573, 582.) The demurrer is overruled as to the Eighteenth [failure to state a claim for punitive damages] and Nineteenth [good faith] affirmative defenses.

2. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the Sixth [release/waiver/estoppel], Eleventh [statute of limitations], Seventeenth [res judicata] and Twentieth [failure to exhaust administrative remedies] affirmative defenses because they fail to plead sufficient facts to constitute a defense. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 430.20(a). (See FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384 [defenses must be pleaded with same detail as claims in complaint].)

3. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the Twelfth [laches] and Twenty-First [workers' compensation exclusivity] affirmative defenses because they are unopposed (as to the Twelfth) and fail as a matter of law. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 430.20(a).)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
